nodes.name: Run Profiler
nodes.notes: Photo received -> hand off to Workflow 2 (Profiler). Set the workflow ID after importing Haq_2_Profiler.

nodes.name: ASR Transcribe
nodes.notes: Replace with Bhashini / Sarvam / Google STT endpoint. Expected response: { transcript, language }. For the hackathon you can also run a tiny local service that downloads the Telegram voice file and calls the ASR API.

parameters.jsCode:
const prev = $('Normalize Update').first().json;
return [{ json: { ...prev, text: $json.transcript || '[voice unintelligible]', detected_language: $json.language || 'hi' } }];

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO users (tg_chat_id, name, language)
VALUES ($1, $2, COALESCE($3, 'hi'))
ON CONFLICT (tg_chat_id) DO UPDATE SET name = COALESCE(users.name, EXCLUDED.name)
RETURNING id, language, profile;

nodes.name: Classify Intent (LLM)
nodes.notes: Gemini flash-lite (cheapest) for high-volume intent routing. Paste your Gemini API key into the URL after key=.

parameters.query: UPDATE users SET profile = profile || $1::jsonb WHERE id = $2 RETURNING id;

SELECT a.id, a.state, a.app_number, s.config->'name'->>'hi' AS scheme_hi, s.config->'name'->>'en' AS scheme_en,
  (SELECT json_agg(json_build_object('at', e.created_at, 'kind', e.kind, 'payload', e.payload) ORDER BY e.created_at)
   FROM events e WHERE e.application_id = a.id) AS timeline
FROM applications a JOIN schemes s ON s.id = a.scheme_id
WHERE a.user_id = $1 ORDER BY a.id DESC LIMIT 3;

WITH app AS (
  INSERT INTO applications (user_id, scheme_id, state, deadline_at, tier)
  SELECT $1, (SELECT (payload->>'scheme_id') FROM events WHERE user_id = $1 AND kind = 'matches_offered' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1), 'ready', now(), NULL
  RETURNING id
)
INSERT INTO events (application_id, user_id, kind, payload)
SELECT id, $1, 'consent', jsonb_build_object('via', 'telegram') FROM app
RETURNING application_id;

nodes.name: Create Application
nodes.notes: Consent creates the application for the top scheme last offered by the Matcher (logged as a 'matches_offered' event). Required docs check is simplified to 'ready' for V0.

nodes.name: Forward OTP to Worker
nodes.notes: Worker keys jobs by application_id. The Executor stores the active job id in users.profile.active_job_id when it dispatches Tier-2 — keep that convention.

parameters.text: ={{ $json.reply || 'Theek hai! Main aapki sarkari yojanaon mein madad karta hoon. Apne baare mein bataiye, ya rashan card ki photo bhejiye. Main kabhi paise nahi maangunga.' }}
